testExecution.configureServerUrlSettings: Configure the server URL in project settings
loadTest.totalRequests.help: Total number of HTTP requests to be sent during the test. Higher numbers take longer but produce more representative results.
loadTest.concurrency.help: Maximum number of requests sent simultaneously. Higher values simulate more users accessing the API at the same time, increasing server load.
loadTest.rampUp.help: Time in seconds to gradually increase concurrency to the maximum value. With 0s, all connections open at once. Higher ramp-up simulates more realistic traffic growth.
loadTest.corsWarning: Execution via Fetch API in the browser. APIs must support CORS to work correctly.
loadTest.e2eWarning: We recommend running an End-to-End test first to validate your pipeline before load testing.
loadTest.wavePoints.help: Timeline points for the load wave. Time is in milliseconds and intensity is a percentage of each runner's safe RPS capacity.
loadTest.wavePoints.hint: Set the test duration, then shape the wave directly on the graph. Click to add a point; drag points to change time and intensity.
loadTest.duration.help: Total wave duration. This defines the timeline width of the graph.
loadTest.targetRps.help: Total desired request rate for the whole load test. Kubernetes reservations use this value to estimate how many runners to prepare.
loadTest.provisioning.title: Provisioning Kubernetes runners
loadTest.provisioning.subtitle: {{ready}} of {{requested}} runners ready

pipelineCreator.typeToPrev: Type the {{format}} of the pipeline to preview
pipelineDocs.title: Pipeline Documentation
pipelineDocs.subtitle: Complete guide on JSON/YAML structure and available variables.
pipelineDocs.structureDesc: A pipeline defines a sequence of HTTP requests to be executed in order.
pipelineDocs.stepMethod: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH or DELETE
pipelineDocs.stepAsserts: Array of assertions to validate the response (optional)
pipelineDocs.stepDelay: Wait time in milliseconds before executing (0–300000, optional)
pipelineDocs.stepRetry: Number of retries on error (0–10, optional)
pipelineDocs.assertionsDesc: Each step can have an asserts array to automatically validate the response. If any assertion fails, the step is marked as error.
pipelineDocs.opExists: Field exists and is not null
pipelineDocs.opNotExists: Field does not exist or is null
pipelineDocs.delayRetryDesc: Each step can configure a wait time before execution and automatic retries on failure.
pipelineDocs.delayDesc: The delay field defines how many milliseconds to wait before executing the step. Useful for waiting for async processing or rate limits.
pipelineDocs.delayRange: Value in milliseconds, from 0 to 300000 (5 minutes).
pipelineDocs.retryDesc: The retry field defines how many times to re-execute the step on error. Total executions will be retry + 1 (original attempt + retries).
pipelineDocs.delayRetryExampleDesc: In this example, the step waits 10 seconds and tries up to 6 times (1 original + 5 retries) until the job is complete.
pipelineDocs.systemVariablesDesc: Use the syntax {{template}} to dynamically interpolate values.
pipelineDocs.envUrlDesc: References the URL of an environment configured in a project's OpenAPI spec. The slug identifies the spec and the env the environment (e.g.: hml, prd).
pipelineDocs.prevStepsDesc: Accesses data from a previous step's response body.
store.loadProjectsError: Error loading projects from backend. Using local data.
store.configureServerUrl: Configure the server URL in project settings to run tests.

chat.title: Test Assistant
chat.welcome: 👋 Hi! I'm the test assistant. Use the app buttons or tell me what you need — I can create, edit, run, and manage your pipelines.
chat.apiKeyRequired: Set your OpenAI API Key in settings to use the assistant.
chat.pipelineSaveError:
❌ **Error saving pipeline "{{name}}" to backend:**
```
{{error}}
```
Check the orchestrator connection and try again.
chat.pipelineValidationErrorMsg:
❌ Pipeline validation errors:
```
{{errors}}
```
I'll fix them and try again...
chat.scenariosIntro: Here are the suggested test scenarios. Select the ones you want to include in the pipeline:
chat.deleteConfirm: 🗑️ Requesting removal of pipeline **"{{name}}"**. Confirm below:
chat.runningE2E: ▶️ Running E2E test for pipeline **"{{name}}"**... Follow the results in the left panel.
chat.runningLoadTest: ⚡ Starting load test for pipeline **"{{name}}"**... Follow the results in the left panel.
chat.noPipelines: No pipelines found in the project.
chat.generateFromScenarios:
Generate the final pipeline with the following selected scenarios. Use the save_pipeline tool to save.

{{list}}

guide.start.title: Start with the essentials
guide.start.description: Previa helps you install, start, and open your local environment quickly. Use the menu to navigate the main steps whenever you need to review the flow.
guide.start.flowDescription: Install the CLI, start the environment, and open the app to verify everything is working.
guide.start.whenDescription: On the first run, on new machines, or when you want to share the setup with the team.
guide.install.title: Install Previa
guide.install.description: Run the command below in your terminal to download and install Previa in your local environment.
guide.install.tip1: Run it in a terminal with permission to install local tools.
guide.install.tip2: Wait for the installation to finish before proceeding to the first run.
guide.install.tip3: If needed, restart the terminal to make sure the command is available.
guide.firstRun.title: Start and open the environment
guide.firstRun.description: After installation, these are the two essential commands to start the environment and open the Previa interface.
guide.more.title: Go deeper
guide.more.description: If you want to explore more details, documentation, and updates, use the official links below.
guide.more.tip: Tip: keep this guide as a quick reference for onboarding new team members.
unsavedChanges.title: Unsaved changes
unsavedChanges.description: You have unsaved changes. Would you like to save before leaving?
